reports.suggestion_message:
to solve this problem, you can try the following approaches:

- update to a newer version to see if the issue has been fixed
  - smithay v0.3.0 has the following newer versions available: 0.4.0, 0.4.1, 0.4.2, 0.4.3, 0.4.4, 0.5.0, 0.5.1, 0.6.0, 0.7.0

- ensure the maintainers know of this problem (e.g. creating a bug report if needed)
or even helping with a fix (e.g. by creating a pull request)
  - smithay@0.3.0
  - repository: https://github.com/Smithay/smithay
  - detailed warning command: `cargo report future-incompatibilities --id 1 --package smithay@0.3.0`

- use your own version of the dependency with the `[patch]` section in `Cargo.toml`
For more information, see:
https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/overriding-dependencies.html#the-patch-section

per_package.smithay@0.3.0:
The package `smithay v0.3.0` currently triggers the following future incompatibility lints:
> [1m[33mwarning[0m[1m: trailing semicolon in macro used in expression position[0m
>    [1m[94m--> [0m/home/mathe/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/smithay-0.3.0/src/backend/egl/native.rs:47:10
>     [1m[94m|[0m
> [1m[94m 47[0m [1m[94m|[0m         );
>     [1m[94m|[0m          [1m[33m^[0m
> [1m[94m...[0m
> [1m[94m138[0m [1m[94m|[0m             egl_platform!(PLATFORM_GBM_KHR, self.as_raw(), &["EGL_KHR_platform_gbm"]),
>     [1m[94m|[0m             [1m[94m-------------------------------------------------------------------------[0m [1m[94min this macro invocation[0m
>     [1m[94m|[0m
>     [1m[94m= [0m[1mwarning[0m: this was previously accepted by the compiler but is being phased out; it will become a hard error in a future release!
>     [1m[94m= [0m[1mnote[0m: for more information, see issue #79813 <https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/79813>
>     [1m[94m= [0m[1mnote[0m: macro invocations at the end of a block are treated as expressions
>     [1m[94m= [0m[1mnote[0m: to ignore the value produced by the macro, add a semicolon after the invocation of `egl_platform`
>     [1m[94m= [0m[1mnote[0m: this warning originates in the macro `egl_platform` (in Nightly builds, run with -Z macro-backtrace for more info)
>
